mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 220 in the CPIAUCSL series triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as bond yields rise and equity valuations contract.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220 despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and residential construction (HOUST) signals robust demand conditions, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with simultaneous GDPC1 and HOUST growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 9% combined with rising payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates tight labor markets, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days due to inflationary pressures and rate hike expectations.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE < 9% and PAYEMS > 130,000.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects strong manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through improved corporate margins and economic confidence.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of INDPRO growth are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential sales (RSAFS) indicates strong household demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days through increased consumer spending and sector-specific earnings growth.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 40% of instances where RSAFS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of falling FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL signals stagflationary risks, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as markets price in both inflation and recession.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods with FEDFUNDS decline and CPIAUCSL rise are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 130,000 signals strong labor market health, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through improved consumer confidence and corporate hiring.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 130,000.
